name: Roly-Poly Peach Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Peeled and sliced Fresh peaches
recipeInstructions:
1. Arrange peaches in an even layer in a shallow 2-quart baking dish.
2. Sprinkle flour, brown sugar, granulated sugar, salt, lemon juice, nutmeg, and almond extract on top of the peaches.
3. Place the dish in a preheated oven set to 450 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for about 10 minutes, or until hot. Preheating allows the fruit mixture and cinnamon roll topping to cook evenly, preventing undercooked fruit or overcooked dough.
4. In a medium bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, salt, and 1/4 cup of sugar. Cut in chilled butter until the mixture forms fine crumbs.
5. Add milk and mix until a soft dough forms. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to a 10x12 inch rectangle.
6. Brush the surface with melted butter and sprinkle with the remaining sugar mixed with cinnamon and nutmeg.
7. Roll from the long side in a jelly roll fashion. Cut into 1-inch slices.
8. Place the slices on top of the hot peach mixture.
9. Bake at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for an additional 10 minutes, then reduce the heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for 20 minutes longer, or until the cinnamon rolls are done.
10. Serve warm or chilled with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream.
